events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON. Need analyze schedule. Let's parse image. Activities with durations and predecessors. Early-start bars. Disruption: Activity M (Tape & finish) needs 3 extra workdays — rework discovered at handoff. Need project finish delay in workdays, whether completion slips, what drives answer.

We need compute original project duration and new duration after M duration increases by 3. Determine if float absorbs. Need identify critical path. Let's reconstruct schedule.

Activities:
A Mobilize & layout [4d] no pred (start 0-4)
B Underground rough-in [2d] pred A. Start 4-6.
C Slab pour [2d] pred B. Start 6-8.
CC Slab cure [3d] pred C. Hatched lag 8-11.
D Framing [5d] pred CC. Start 11-16.
E Roof dry-in [2d] pred D. Start 16-18.
F Electrical rough [5d] pred E. Start 18-23.
G Plumbing top-out [4d] pred E. Start 18-22.
H HVAC rough [5d] pred E. Start 18-23.
I Rough inspection [1d] pred F,G,H. Start max(23,22,23)=23-24.
J Insulation [1d] pred I. Start 24-25.
K Insulation inspection [1d] pred J. Start 25-26.
L Drywall hang [4d] pred K. Start 26-30.
M Tape & finish [4d] pred L. Start 30-34.
N Paint [1d] pred M. Start 34-35.
O Trim carpentry [2d] pred N. Start 35-37.
P Flooring [3d] pred N. Start 35-38.
Q Final inspection [1d] pred O,P. Start max(37,38)=38-39.

Original finish workday 39 (end of day 39? bar ends at 39). Actually x-axis goes to 40. Q bar from 38 to 39. So project duration 39 workdays.

Now M increases from 4 to 7 days. M start remains 30 (pred L finish 30). New finish 37. Then N starts 37-38. O starts 38-40. P starts 38-41. Q starts max(40,41)=41-42. Project finish 42. Delay = 3 workdays. Since M is on critical path (no float), completion slips by 3 days.

Need identify what drives answer: M is on critical path; successor O and P and final Q are driven; no float. Also note M rework discovered at handoff means work in progress? Stage in-progress. Need output JSON with required fields. Need decide decision? This is schedule impact assessment, not pass/fail inspection. The task: decide what work can proceed, what is blocked, and what recovery plan is valid. But required output shape includes decision pass/fail/needs_more_info. Since schedule disruption causes delay but not necessarily dangerous/non-compliant. However rework discovered at handoff could be defective work. But component condition categories none flagged. The question asks project finish delay. We can set decision maybe "needs_more_info"? Let's interpret. The scenario: assessing real trade work. We need decide what work can proceed, blocked, recovery. The disruption: M needs 3 extra workdays. At point observed, M is in progress (rework discovered at handoff). Work that can proceed: Activities not dependent on M and not yet completed? Let's identify current in-progress stage. The schedule is lookahead; disruption at M. If M is currently in progress and delayed, then N, O, P, Q are blocked until M completes. Workable activities are those whose predecessors are satisfied and not blocked by M? But schedule is early-start; if we are at time around M (workday 30-34 originally), many earlier activities completed. The question specifically: "Use workable for a list of activity IDs when the task asks what work can still start." The task says decide what work can proceed, what is blocked, and what recovery plan is valid. So include workable list maybe activities that can still proceed unaffected by M delay: all predecessors of M are done, so nothing upstream can start (already done). Downstream N,O,P,Q blocked. Parallel? There are no parallel activities independent after M except maybe none. But if we consider current time before M delay, M itself can proceed (rework). Also maybe if there is float elsewhere? F/G/H have some float? Let's compute floats.

Original early start/finish:
A 0-4
B 4-6
C 6-8
CC 8-11
D 11-16
E 16-18
F 18-23
G 18-22
H 18-23
I 23-24
J 24-25
K 25-26
L 26-30
M 30-34
N 34-35
O 35-37
P 35-38
Q 38-39

Late times from end 39 backward:
Q LF 39, L…
